an-nam DU₁₁.GA-ma uš-⸢ken₂-ma?⸣ x [...]

(o 14') He says this and prostrates himself and pays ... [...] in front of Šamaš and [...].

o 15'15'

ana IGI dUTU i-ša₂-qal u₃ [...]

o 16'16'

dUTU an-nu-u KU₃.BABBAR ip-ṭi₂-ri-šu d⸢UTU⸣ [...]

(o 16') Šamaš, this (is) the silver for his ransom. Šamaš, [...].

o 17'17'

⸢lu₂⸣MAŠ.MAŠ ana IGI KI.MAḪ gišRIN₂ IL₂-⸢ma?⸣ [...]

(o 17') The mašmaššu-priest raises the scales in front of the grave and [...].

o 18'18'

⸢lu₂⸣MAŠ.MAŠ LU₂ ša₂ KI.MAḪ ⸢DU₃⸣uš US₂ KI.⸢MAḪ⸣ [...]

(o 18') The mašmaššu-priest (and) the man who is building the grave [...] the side of the grave.

o 19'19'

⸢ki⸣-is-pa ana dA.NUN.NA.⸢KE⸣ i-kas₂-sip GA x [...]

(o 19') He makes a funerary offering to the Anunnaku. [...] milk, ... [...].

o 20'20'

NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A LAL₃ I₃.NUN.NA GARan lu₂MAŠ.⸢MAŠ⸣ [...]

(o 20') He sets in place a cake (made) of honey and butter. The mašmaššu-priest [...].

o 21'21'

ana IGI dA.NUN.NA.KE UR₅.⸢GIM⸣ [...]

(o 21') [...] as follows in front of the Anunnaku.

o 22'22'

mu-uḫ-ra di-gi₄-gi₄ dA.⸢NUN⸣.NA.⸢KE⸣ AN? [...]

(o 22') Accept (this offering), Igigi (and) Anunnaku, ... [...],

o 23'23'

⸢a⸣-šib ina DU₆.KU₃ KIti₃ DAGALti₃ ša₂ ina AN⸢e⸣ x [...]

(o 23') living on the Duku in the wide Underworld, who [...] in heaven ...,

o 24'24'

ina KIti₃ KI.TUŠ-su-nu gal-ta-at [...]

(o 24') whose dwelling in the Underworld is fearsome. [...], your servant who is building the grave, am frightened and ... [...].

o 25'25'

⸢ARAD-ku⸣-nu ša₂ KI.MAḪ DU₃uš pal-ḫa-ku-ma x [...]

o 26'26'

[x x] ⸢KU₃⸣ mu-uḫ-ra-ma [...]

o 27'27'

[... a]-⸢a⸣ uš-ta-ni-iḫ a-a uš-ta-x [...]

(o 27') [...]. May he not be depressed. May he not .... [...].

o 28'28'

[...] x LAGAB? i-dir-tu₄ a-a DAB-su ⸢KI.MAḪ⸣ ŠA₃? [...]

(o 28') [...] ... misery [...]. May the grave not seize him. ... [...].

o 29'29'

[...] x IGI ḪUL SAG.ḪUL.ḪA.ZA a-a TE-šu₂ ma-mit [...]

(o 29') [...] ... the evil eye (and) evil spirit [...]. May a curse, [...] (and) an unfavourable utterance not approach him. May sorcery, spells, [...], evil [...], [...] ... [...] not approach him.

o 30'30'

r 5'5'

(r 5') [...] ... (which) you have laid, [...],

r 6'6'

[... mu]-⸢un⸣-tag-ga irigal ki ba₉-⸢ra₂⸣-[ta ...]

(r 6') [...] (which) you have laid, [...] from the Underworld, the remote place,

r 7'7'

[...] ⸢d⸣en-lil₂-la₂ uš₈ ⸢e₂?-a?⸣ [...]

(r 7') [...] of Enlil, the foundation of the house [...],

r 8'8'

[...] x dkug-sud šag₄ kur ⸢maḫ⸣ [...]

(r 8') [...] .... After Kugsud, [...] inside the magnificent underworld, has thrown into the holy water vessel tamarisk, innuš-plant, [...] palm, [...], naŋasi'e-plant, silver, gold, nir-stone, carnelian, [...], red [...], cedar oil, good oil, honey, butter, [...], cedar, cypress (and) zahad-wood, [...].

r 9'9'

[a-gub₂]-⸢ba⸣ ŋeššinig ŋešin-nu-uš ⸢ŋešŋešnimbar?⸣ [...]

r 10'10'

⸢naŋa?⸣-si kug-babbar kug-sig₁₇ na₄nir₂ na₄⸢gug⸣ [...]

r 11'11'

[x] ḫuš i₃ ŋešeren i₃ dug₃-ga ⸢lal₃⸣ i₃-nun-⸢na⸣ [...]

r 12'12'

[ŋeš]⸢eren⸣ ŋeššur-men₅ ŋešza-ḫadad šag₄-⸢ba⸣ u-me-⸢ni-šub⸣ [...]

r 13'13'

[ki]-⸢maḫ⸣ mu-un-du₃-a suḫuš-bi irigal-ta [...]

(r 13') The foundations of the grave which you have built [...] from the underworld.

r 14'14'

⸢d⸣en-ki lugal abzu-ke₄ suḫuš-⸢bi irigal⸣-ta ⸢ba₉?⸣-[ra₂? ...]

(r 14') Enki, king of the Abzu, its foundations [...] from the remote Underworld.

r 15'15'

e₂ kur-ra ḫe₂-me-en e₂ sig₉-⸢sig₉-ga⸣ ḫe₂-me-en [...]

(r 15') May you be the house of the Underworld. May you be the deeply silent house. [...].

r 16'16'

e₂ ŋarza-zu kur-ra ZAG x [x ḫe₂]-me-en AN x [...]

(r 16') Your house of rites (is) in the Underworld. May you be ... [...]. ... [...].

r 17'17'

dasar-lu₂-ḫi ⸢dumu eridug⸣[ki-ga-ke₄] x x [...]

(r 17') Asalluhi, the child of Eridug, ... [...].

r 18'18'

mi-ni-in-kug mi-ni-⸢in-sikil⸣ mi-ni-[in-dadag ...]

(r 18') He has made it pure there, made it shine there, made it bright there, [...].

r 19'19'

⸢lu₂⸣MAŠ.MAŠ ana IGI KEŠ₂ ⸢an-nam⸣ ŠID-ma ⸢GIM⸣ [...]

(r 19') The mašmaššu-priest recites this in front of the ritual assemblage and, when [...], [...] the foundations with the shovel, ... places [...]

r 20'20'

⸢giš⸣MAR SUḪUŠ [...] x KI-MEŠ [...]

x x x [...] x [x] x [...]
